methodology_sections: 1. Lifecycle Integrity - State transitions, illegal states, predecessors
2. Monotonicity & Irreversibility - Increasing values, one-way transitions
3. Access & Permission Logic - Conditional access, role requirements
4. Time & Resource Integrity - Time-locks, resource bounds, expiry

invariants.description: onSetAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips hook's returndatasize check evaluates BEFORE the external call, using stale returndatasize from a prior balanceOf call (32 bytes). Since 32 < 64, the or() condition always evaluates to true, causing the function to revert even on successful hook calls.
invariants.attack_scenario: Any market with useOnSetAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips enabled will be unable to change annual interest rate or reserve ratio. The borrower cannot adjust market parameters, potentially trapping lenders in unfavorable terms or preventing the borrower from reducing reserve ratios when needed. This is a permanent DoS on a critical configuration function.
invariants.invariant_logic: In assembly: `or(lt(returndatasize(), 0x40), iszero(call(...)))` — returndatasize() is the RETURNDATASIZE opcode evaluated BEFORE the CALL opcode. After _getUpdatedState(), last external call is balanceOf() returning 32 bytes, so lt(32, 64) == true, and or(true, X) == true regardless of call success.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_check() public { /* Deploy market with onSetAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips enabled. Call setAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips — it will always revert even if hooks contract returns valid data */ }

invariants.description: The scale factor must be monotonically non-decreasing across all state updates. It represents accumulated compound interest and is the conversion rate between scaled and normalized balances. A decrease would directly reduce the value of all lender token holdings.
invariants.attack_scenario: If scaleFactor could decrease (e.g., through arithmetic overflow in updateScaleFactorAndFees or through a manipulated timestamp), all lender balances would silently lose value. A borrower's debt would be reduced while lenders' claims diminish, effectively stealing from lenders.
invariants.invariant_logic: For every call to _getUpdatedState() or _calculateCurrentState(): new_state.scaleFactor >= old_state.scaleFactor
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_scaleFactorMonotonic() public view { MarketState memory current = _state; /* after any state transition: assert(newState.scaleFactor >= current.scaleFactor); */ }

invariants.description: Market closure (isClosed = true) must be irreversible. Once a market is closed, no function should be able to set isClosed back to false. All deposit, borrow, and repay operations must be blocked on closed markets, while withdrawals must remain executable.
invariants.attack_scenario: If isClosed could be toggled back to false, a borrower could close a market (setting APR to 0, reserveRatio to 10000, timeDelinquent to 0), let lenders withdraw, then reopen the market in a manipulated state where interest tracking has been reset, defrauding remaining lenders.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall state transitions: old_state.isClosed == true => new_state.isClosed == true; isClosed == true => deposit() reverts AND borrow() reverts AND repay() reverts; isClosed == true => executeWithdrawal() still succeeds
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_closedIrreversible() public view { if (_state.isClosed) { /* assert no function can set _state.isClosed = false */ assert(_state.isClosed == true); } }

invariants.description: The sum of all account scaled balances must always equal scaledTotalSupply minus scaledPendingWithdrawals. This is the fundamental accounting invariant ensuring no tokens are created or destroyed outside of authorized operations.
invariants.attack_scenario: If this invariant breaks, either tokens are minted from nothing (diluting lenders) or tokens disappear (locking funds). For example, a rounding error in transfer/deposit/withdrawal that systematically favors one direction could gradually desynchronize these values, leading to insolvency or trapped funds.
invariants.invariant_logic: sum(account.scaledBalance for all accounts) == state.scaledTotalSupply - state.scaledPendingWithdrawals
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_balanceAccounting() public view { /* uint256 totalBalances = sum of all _accounts[addr].scaledBalance; assert(totalBalances == _state.scaledTotalSupply - _state.scaledPendingWithdrawals); */ }

invariants.description: When decreasing reserveRatioBips, the market must already satisfy the OLD (higher) liquidity requirement. When increasing reserveRatioBips, the market must satisfy the NEW (higher) liquidity requirement after the change. Both annualInterestBips and reserveRatioBips are set between the two checks, so the order of validation is critical.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the ordering of checks is wrong, a borrower could decrease the reserve ratio while the market is delinquent under the current ratio, then borrow the freed-up reserves before lenders can react, leaving the market undercollateralized.
invariants.invariant_logic: reserveRatioBips_new < reserveRatioBips_old => liquidityRequired(old_ratio) <= totalAssets(); reserveRatioBips_new > reserveRatioBips_old => liquidityRequired(new_ratio) <= totalAssets()
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_reserveRatioChange() public view { /* After setAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips: assert(state.liquidityRequired() <= totalAssets()); */ }

invariants.description: Borrower's sanction check for borrow() must use raw Chainalysis flag (isFlaggedByChainalysis), NOT the overridable isSanctioned. This prevents a borrower from overriding their own sanction status to extract funds from the market while sanctioned.
invariants.attack_scenario: If borrow() used isSanctioned instead of isFlaggedByChainalysis, a sanctioned borrower could call overrideSanction(borrower) on the sentinel (where msg.sender == borrower), then call borrow() to drain the market while sanctions are technically active on Chainalysis.
invariants.invariant_logic: borrow() calls _isFlaggedByChainalysis(borrower) which queries Chainalysis directly, not _isSanctioned which factors in sanctionOverrides[borrower]
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_borrowSanctionCheck() public view { /* assert that borrow() path uses isFlaggedByChainalysis, not isSanctioned */ }

invariants.description: normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals must always accurately reflect the total amount of underlying assets reserved for processed but unclaimed withdrawal batches. Over-counting locks excess funds; under-counting allows borrower to steal reserved withdrawal funds.
invariants.attack_scenario: If normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals is inflated (e.g., by double-counting a batch payment), borrowableAssets decreases and legitimate borrows are blocked. If deflated, the borrower can borrow assets that should be reserved for lender withdrawals, leaving withdrawing lenders unable to claim their funds.
invariants.invariant_logic: state.normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals == sum(batch.normalizedAmountPaid - sum(status.normalizedAmountWithdrawn for all accounts) for all processed batches)
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_unclaimedWithdrawals() public view { /* For each batch that is fully or partially paid: sum(amountPaid - amountWithdrawn) should equal normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals */ }

invariants.description: Withdrawal batches must be processed in strict FIFO order from the unpaidBatches queue. Earlier batches must be fully paid before later batches receive any payment. Only the first unpaid batch is processed at a time.
invariants.attack_scenario: If FIFO ordering is violated, a later batch could be paid before an earlier one, allowing some lenders to front-run others. An attacker could create a new withdrawal, get it paid from available liquidity while earlier withdrawals remain unpaid, effectively stealing priority from earlier withdrawers.
invariants.invariant_logic: For all i < j in unpaidBatches: batch[i].scaledAmountBurned < batch[i].scaledTotalAmount implies batch[j].scaledAmountBurned == 0 (later batches cannot be partially paid while earlier ones are unpaid)
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_fifoWithdrawals() public view { /* uint32[] memory expiries = _withdrawalData.unpaidBatches.values(); for (uint i = 0; i < expiries.length - 1; i++) { assert(_withdrawalData.batches[expiries[i]].scaledAmountBurned == _withdrawalData.batches[expiries[i]].scaledTotalAmount || _withdrawalData.batches[expiries[i+1]].scaledAmountBurned == 0); } */ }

invariants.description: Withdrawal execution must be blocked until batch expiry UNLESS the market is closed. When the market is closed, all withdrawals from any batch (including non-expired ones) must be immediately executable to prevent fund lockup.
invariants.attack_scenario: If early execution were allowed on open markets, a lender could bypass the withdrawal batch duration, undermining the borrower's liquidity management. Conversely, if execution were blocked on closed markets, lenders could have funds trapped for the full batch duration even though the market is done.
invariants.invariant_logic: executeWithdrawal(account, expiry) succeeds implies (block.timestamp > expiry OR state.isClosed == true)
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_withdrawalTiming() public view { /* For any successful executeWithdrawal(account, expiry): assert(expiry < block.timestamp || _state.isClosed); */ }

invariants.description: closeMarket must fully resolve all pending withdrawal batches (scaledPendingWithdrawals == 0) or revert. The borrower must deposit sufficient funds to cover all debts before closure, ensuring no lender has unresolvable pending withdrawals.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a market could close with non-zero scaledPendingWithdrawals, those pending withdrawals would never accrue further interest (APR set to 0) and might never be fully paid, causing lender losses. The borrower could selectively close markets to escape obligations on pending batches.
invariants.invariant_logic: After successful closeMarket(): state.scaledPendingWithdrawals == 0 AND state.isClosed == true AND state.annualInterestBips == 0 AND state.reserveRatioBips == 10000 AND state.timeDelinquent == 0
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_closeMarketComplete() public view { if (_state.isClosed) { assert(_state.annualInterestBips == 0); assert(_state.reserveRatioBips == 10000); } }

invariants.description: Total assets in the market must always be sufficient to cover normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals. Protocol fee collection must respect this priority: unclaimed withdrawals take precedence over protocol fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: If collectFees could drain assets below normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals, lenders who have already had their withdrawal batches processed would be unable to execute their withdrawals. The fee recipient would extract funds that belong to lenders.
invariants.invariant_logic: After collectFees(): totalAssets() >= state.normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals; withdrawableProtocolFees = min(totalAssets - normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals, accruedProtocolFees)

invariants.description: lastInterestAccruedTimestamp must be monotonically non-decreasing and must be updated to block.timestamp on every state-modifying call. This prevents double-accrual of interest for the same time period.
invariants.attack_scenario: If lastInterestAccruedTimestamp could go backwards or not be updated, interest could be accrued multiple times for the same period. An attacker could manipulate transaction ordering to cause the same interest to be added twice, inflating the scale factor and allowing lenders to withdraw more than deposited.
invariants.invariant_logic: After _writeState: state.lastInterestAccruedTimestamp == block.timestamp; For consecutive state updates: new_timestamp >= old_timestamp

invariants.description: The borrow function must enforce that the amount borrowed does not exceed borrowableAssets, which accounts for reserve ratio requirements, pending withdrawals, unclaimed withdrawals, and protocol fees. After a successful borrow, liquidityRequired must not exceed totalAssets.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the borrower could borrow more than borrowableAssets, the market becomes immediately delinquent, penalty APR kicks in, and lenders who try to withdraw may find insufficient liquidity. In extreme cases, the market could become insolvent.
invariants.invariant_logic: borrow(amount) succeeds implies amount <= state.borrowableAssets(totalAssets()); After borrow: state.liquidityRequired() <= totalAssets() (assuming no instant delinquency)
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_borrowLimit() public view { /* uint256 borrowable = currentState().borrowableAssets(totalAssets()); assert(any borrow amount <= borrowable); */ }

invariants.description: Only one pending withdrawal batch can exist at a time (identified by pendingWithdrawalExpiry). A new batch can only be created when pendingWithdrawalExpiry is 0. When a batch expires, it must be processed and pendingWithdrawalExpiry reset to 0 before a new batch can be created.
invariants.attack_scenario: If multiple pending batches could exist simultaneously, the accounting for scaledPendingWithdrawals would be corrupted. Batch payments could be applied incorrectly, and the FIFO ordering invariant would break.
invariants.invariant_logic: state.pendingWithdrawalExpiry != 0 implies exactly one active pending batch exists at that expiry; New batch creation requires pendingWithdrawalExpiry == 0
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_singlePendingBatch() public view { uint32 expiry = _state.pendingWithdrawalExpiry; if (expiry != 0) { assert(_withdrawalData.batches[expiry].scaledTotalAmount > 0); } }

invariants.description: Only registered borrowers on the arch-controller can deploy markets or hooks instances. The market must be deployed through the factory and registered with the arch-controller. Salt must contain the deployer's address (or be zero-prefixed) to prevent front-running of market deployment.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an unregistered borrower could deploy a market, they could create markets without proper vetting. If salt is not tied to the sender, an attacker could front-run a borrower's deployment transaction by using the same salt, deploying the market under their control or causing the legitimate deployment to fail.
invariants.invariant_logic: deployMarket(params) succeeds implies archController.isRegisteredBorrower(msg.sender) AND (bytes20(salt) == msg.sender OR bytes20(salt) == bytes20(0))
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_marketDeploymentAuth() public view { /* All markets in _marketsByHooksTemplate were deployed by registered borrowers with valid salts */ }

invariants.description: Escrowed assets can only be released when the account is no longer sanctioned (either removed from Chainalysis or overridden by borrower). The escrow's canReleaseEscrow check must query current sanction status, not a cached value.
invariants.attack_scenario: If escrow release did not check current sanction status, a sanctioned user could release escrowed funds while still sanctioned, violating sanctions compliance. If the check used stale data, there could be a window where sanctioned users can extract funds.
invariants.invariant_logic: releaseEscrow() succeeds implies isSanctioned(borrower, account) == false at time of call; releaseEscrow() transfers entire balance to account
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_escrowRelease() public view { /* assert(canReleaseEscrow() == !IWildcatSanctionsSentinel(sentinel).isSanctioned(borrower, account)); */ }
